name: Lepton universality in b -> c l nu: R(D), R(D*)
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the world-average ratios R(D) = Br(B -> D tau nu)/Br(B -> D l nu) and R(D*) = Br(B -> D* tau nu)/Br(B -> D* l nu). The HFLAV 2024 averages sit ~3 sigma above SM predictions, a long-standing tension testing tau-vs-light-lepton universality in the charged-current b -> c transition.

regime.description: B-meson semileptonic decays via tree-level W exchange; sensitivity to TeV-scale leptoquarks, charged Higgs, or W' bosons coupling preferentially to the third lepton family.

current_bound.experiment: HFLAV 2024 world average (BaBar, Belle, Belle II, LHCb)
current_bound.reference: HFLAV Collaboration, 'Averages of b-hadron, c-hadron, and tau-lepton properties as of 2023', arXiv:2411.18639 (HFLAV 2024 update; see also HFLAV web tables)
current_bound.confidence_level: Combined tension ~3.3 sigma against SM predictions

what_it_excludes: Provides motivation for leptoquark models (U_1 vector LQ in particular), charged-Higgs models, and W' bosons. Constrains the TeV-scale flavor structure of any framework predicting third-generation-enhanced charged currents. The persistence of this anomaly across multiple experiments and channels is one of the strongest current hints of TeV-scale flavor BSM.

parameterization.name: Weak effective theory at the b-quark scale: Wilson coefficients C_V_L, C_V_R, C_S_L, C_S_R, C_T for each lepton flavor in b -> c l nu.
parameterization.reference: Becirevic et al., JHEP 11, 084 (2016); Iguro, Kitahara, Watanabe, arXiv:2210.10751.
parameterization.summary: SM has only C_V_L = 1 for all flavors. Anomalies parameterize a tau-flavor enhancement of C_V_L^tau, or generation of right-handed/scalar/tensor coefficients.

procedural.predicted_observable: R(D), R(D*) = integral over phase space of |amplitude|^2, ratio of tau to l = e, mu channels; sensitive to specific Wilson-coefficient combinations.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Required deviation: C_V_L^tau ~ 1.05 (5% enhancement), or scalar / tensor coefficients of order 0.1.
framework_inputs: Match framework onto WET in b -> c l nu sector at scale m_b.
Use lattice QCD form factors (Fermilab/MILC, JLQCD) to compute R(D), R(D*) in the framework.

primary_references.citation: HFLAV Collaboration, 'Averages of b-hadron, c-hadron, and tau-lepton properties as of 2023', arXiv:2411.18639 (2024)
Iguro, Kitahara, Watanabe, 'Global fit to b -> c tau nu anomalies', arXiv:2210.10751 (2022)
